raw_text: Speedy service, Keep it up baristas! always very friendly and professional.  A little facts on Starbucks...originally, Starbucks offered only two sizes: short (8 ounces) and tall (12 ounces). When the company added grande, venti and trenta, they kept the old names for the original drinks.  At most Starbucks around the world, the size options are: short (8 ounces), tall (12 ounces), grande (16 ounces), venti (24 ounces), and trenta (31 ounces).  Grande is Italian for 'large,' venti means 'twenty,' and trenta is 'thirty'.   After a trip to Italy in 1983, founder Howard Schultz became obsessed with its coffee bars and wanted to introduce Italian names. As customers demanded larger sizes, 'short' was taken off the menu board so the larger sizes of 'venti' and 'trenta' sizes could be added Tall ended up becoming the smallest sized drink on the printed menu.

raw_text: I've been to this location about a dozen times. My husband and I enjoy going in on a rainy day and reading for a bit. The inside it pretty big and they have outdoor seating but it's usually pretty crowded. Unfortunately the last 2/3 visits have been negative. We stopped on a Sunday morning before church and went in because the drive-thru line was super long. I ordered the same thing for both myself and my husband. It took 15 minutes for our drinks to come out. Not kidding. I requested the drinks have sugar-free syrup because of dietary restrictions. We rushed out once the drinks were made and didn't try them until we got to our car and were out of the parking lot. One of the drinks was SF and the other wasn't. We had to throw the one with regular syrup away and couldn't go back because our drinks took so long. The woman who made them was clearly flustered because they were super busy but she made them back-to-back. That mistake shouldn't have been made.  Fast forward to this morning at 6:50 am. No one was in the drive-thru. I ordered my drink (the same one that was messed up previously) and gave the woman my money. A man made my drink and set it on the counter and then went to the back, I think. The woman also disappeared. Literally. FOUR MINUTES PASSED and my drink was sitting there. She came back to take someone else's order in the drive-thru and then proceeded to get a cup and start making their drink. I kindly said "excuse me, I have somewhere I need to be. Can you please give me my drink?" She didn't say anything and just handed it to be. I was in shock. I'm not kidding when I say it was four whole minutes. If I could've reached in the window and grabbed the drink myself I would have.  I tell this long story because both of these poor visits involved the same woman. A little bit older with short blonde hair. She can be very nice but tends to be a little out of it at times. I hate to call someone specific out but in both accounts she was the one to blame. I still plan on visiting this location but hope that this particular employee gets the hang of her job.

raw_text: I love Starbucks. Forget what people say about Dunkin' Donuts--that's bilge water! I don't even drink coffee anymore, but they offer an array of herbal teas iced and hot, and lately I've been treating myself to dark chocolate-covered graham crackers. Don't judge.  That being said, I must concur with many Yelp reviewers in noting that this branch is THE most inefficient location I've ever frequented. First, although the line is routinely interminably long, only ONE register is open. Employees appear to be milling about in the background doing who knows what. True, other baristas are busy filling ridiculously complicated drink orders ("Ummmm...I'll have a half-caf iced Americano with a splash of coconut milk, a smidge of soy, and the tears of the woman behind me in line who's screaming and pulling her hair out."), but still.   The other day, two middle-aged women conferred with the cashier for 15 minutes as I waited (uncharacteristically) my turn patiently. Were they opening an offshore bank account, transferring money from their hedge fund to checking? No; for the life of me, I have no clue what the heck was happening, despite my earnest attempts at eavesdropping. Variations of this scenario are repeated daily, but this is the location closest to me.  The workers here are very friendly, and I usually tip, but I take serious umbrage with the extremely poor service. I kind of enjoy getting worked up about stuff, so I'll totally continue to patronize this Starbucks, but still...

raw_text: I'm not a frequent coffeehouse patronizer, but this Starbucks is one of my go-to locations because its close to work. Located on the ground floor of the Marriott, this Starbucks has a very corporate vibe to it. There is always a ton of people in here, with pretty significant lines.   This is not a cozy-feeling Starbucks at all, being that interior space is very tight--like a thin, lengthy road. The amount of seating is sparse, and rather disjointed (a couple alcoves and a bar-type window seating area). This is not a place that you would wish to spend any length of time in.  However, you can be pretty sure of one thing - your food & drink will be fresh, being that there is a high volume of people that pass through here. In fact, I *highly* recommend that you get the Starbucks app to order/pay so that you can skip the crazy lines.   Theresa may be the best barista in town - we're talking Best of Philly. She obviously works hard, she always has my drinks made perfectly, and she is one of the nicest ladies in town. I think she is only here in the late morning, though.  I tried ordering through the app just yesterday, and it all worked flawlessly for me. I ordered and paid with my mobile phone, it gave me an estimated time of 3-8 minutes that my order would be read, I walked over to the Starbucks, and by golly I had my drink in-hand on the 8th minute!  Why this location is not open 24 hours, is beyond me; apparently Philly cannot sustain a 24 hour Starbucks. If any location was going to be 24 hours, I'd think this would have been it.

Drink quality is inconsistent. Want too much ice? Check! not enough ice? Check! That's probably among the most minor of the drink faux pas i've experienced here.   Canyon cafe is a much smoother transaction, and the pastries are good! They just opened up a starbs in the library, so there's another option for the UA set. If you must have Starbucks, the one on University ave. is much better.

The baristas were busy, so it took a while to get to my order at the counter.  It seemed like a longer wait than usual for them to make my coffee, but I'm a patient person, so I killed time by using the restroom.  After I finally received my drink, I took a few sips, and almost took in a whole coffee bean.  That was a first, and I've been a long time customer.   Since I didn't choke on it, I just stuck it in my napkin and forgot about it, especially since I was engrossed in conversation.

raw_text: Starbucks locations tend to blend together, unless they are blessed with newer renovations, charismatic staff, or Clover (or other fancy coffee programs).  This location is my "grab-a-latte before braving I-69" Starbucks; I think a lot of people have the same perception, as it is always super busy with commuters. Beyond that, there isn't much that differentiates it from other typical locations. The drive-thru can easily back up during prime times, but I've never had my decaf nonfat latte messed up.  The major con for this location is trying to access it when driving to it from any direction but north. I usually have to criss-cross across parking lots to get close.

raw_text: One of my favorite area Starbucks - it's got a great modern interior, plenty of seating, and it moves drinks quickly.  If coming to relax and chill with your drink is the name of your game, then this is the perfect place - there is an area of 6 super-comfy Restoration Hardware style chairs at the front of the store. I always seem to be able to grab one, but I typically come at off-peak times (mid-afternoon; but come to think of it, is there really ever any particular peak times at a coffee house outside of morning rush?).  This location always has some interesting tunes to play in the background, that aren't overwhelming to studying or conversing.  I haven't had any reason to complain about the drinks that the baristas have pumped out, thus far.  The ordering area has a food display case that is usually stocked with most every particular thing that the Starbucks company offers. It probably wouldn't take you long to request something in the case, after perusing it!  Also, there is more than plenty enough parking here; if that's something you're worried about.

raw_text: While I may not be the biggest fan of Starbucks - you know, it's the whole "giant chain" thing. I just prefer to support local! Also, I don't love their cappuccino, the espresso just tastes bitter. Of course you can't taste it in a big, fancy, 900 calorie frappuccino... Another reason why I don't like Starbucks, it's yet another American chain that is geared to getting people f-a-t! But enough of what I don't like about SatrB's and on to what I really like about this location: I just love the location! High Street is so quant, full of adorable shops and free parking. Then there is the patio situated under the gigantic sprawling trees of the east Glenora address - shaded and breezy in the summer, gorgeous in the fall with a plethora of falling leaves. raw_text: Decent Starbucks. Very small parking lot, maybe 5-6 spots. Behind it is a huge Lake & in the winter when it's frozen, it turns into an ice rink. Kids and adults alike, play hockey and twirl around from morning till sunset. They all pile into Starbucks to warm up after, so it's always busy during the winter. There's about 10-12 tables/chairs down one side of the store. There are four leather club chairs by the front window which are a coveted spot. Across the front there's a counter top with some stools. Two big, clean bathrooms in the back, with baby changing stations. The place was clean. My particular barista couldn't have been nicer! There's also additional seating out back, overlooking the scenic lake for the warmer months. The only thing missing is a fireplace. The Newtown location has one, which makes it my favorite location. But, they closed today 1/7/18 for 3 weeks of renovations!! Ahh!! Now, I have to get my caffeine fix someplace else till they reopen. All in all, this location is nice, clean & mobile service is quick.
